id: 6,Does it ever really get better?,"I feel like I've tried it all... My anxiety/depression had me unemployed and stuck at home for years, and now I'm doing everything different than how I did before. I got a job, reached out to people, made connections with a few new people, got a gym membership and go regularly, no longer binge eat/and eat healthily... and it's like STILL. Still, the suicidal thoughts have not gone away. If anything, lately they've been worse. I'm just so tired. Why do this? Why pay bills and make an effort? What's the point of anything? I hate everything, and most people and trust no one truly. Like shit ... will it EVER go away? I'm starting to think suicide is the ONLY answer.",1.0

id: 28,"I finally conviced myself to talk to an adult about my mental health issues. Not a good idea. (sorry for wall of text, I had to put it all in there)","Hello.
First of all I would like to clarify that I am not a native English speaker
title: so grammar errors and typos may occur.

id: Age: 15
Religon: my pranets are and so am I an atheist
To give you some long
title: but important backstory:

id: I should probably start with a kindergarten. Or to be more precise, how I was not in a kindergarten. My mom is extremely toxic towards various people. Not like she wants to, she tries to be a good person all the time. But she tends to be pissed off even because of a smallest things. That caused her to have an argument with the (f)kindergarten principal the second day I was there. I didn't come there any more. And instead of looking for a ""better"" kindergarten (this one was ok already) she just doesn't care or something and keeping me home seemed like a good idea for her. So that was my first disadvantage - I didn't get socialized with others in the early age.
Maybe because of that and because of what weirdo I just am
title: I got really really scared when it was time for the first school day. I mean yes it is stress for everybody
body: but it was like what someone of you would like to call an anxiety attack. I was always ok with strange adults
label: but the idea of being with kids I have never seen before (especially when majority of them were friends from kindergartens already) the whole thing caused me some real stress. Thankfully these were just my concerns and after all I made a good friend with one if the kids and I did just okay.

id: What would come however
title: I could never imagine. My parents couldn't live together anymore and we (me and my brother) couldn't live with our dad. Not like he was beating us or anything like that. But you know... Smoking weed and dropping off work can be both financially intense hobbies.

id: In the middle of the 1st grade
title: the looooong series of moving
body: living in a shitholes and changing schools began. I literally changed school so many times that I can't even remember what all the schools were and how many exactly of them were in total. But I could easily count above 13. Every time coming to the new class of people
label: experiencing that anxiety attack as in the first school day. Also because being single mom is a hard job
column5: we always lived in a literal shithole
column6: with some matrassess usually on the floor
column7: cheap very out-of-fashion clothes and I didn't even get my first cell phone until 8th grade. All of that didn't make the socializing much easier for me.

id: Also what I should probably mention is that I skipped the 3rd grade. Honestly I don't know how to put this so it does not look like bragging
title: but this will be important later on: I am a Mensa member with measured IQ of 143 and this and other factors lead my teachers and mom to conclusion that I should probably skip a grade
body: so I could get more from the education as 3rd grade stuff was too easy for me. That's nice and everything
label: but being not just a strange broke kid but also a one that is 1 or even 2 years younger from everyone else in the class - I hope you can imagine that. That caused me a first situations of being bullied.

id: Not until the 7th grade, where I of course changed the school again, but actually ended up in class with a bunch of very nice people. My mom promised us: ""This is the last time we are moving."" The relief was surreal. The people were actually so nice that I was out the whole day, enjoying company of my group and ended up getting my first fiancГ©. If anything, those were the best years of my life yet.

It is half through the 8th grade and guess what. Mom ran out of money (she btw stole that from our dad and it was a lot of money meant for ME and MY BROTHER personally for good grades) and we were moving. fucking. again. I just didn't know what to do. My mom even being a bitch she is
title: she gave me ultimatums like go or stay here but in a children's home. That's where the first sings of my issues started to show up.

id: Since there were not other things for me to do and since the world started to fall apart for me (we started living with our dad again at this point), I started to fall for video games. At that point and even now, I am not afraid to admit that I'm a video game addict. You could say ""man get a hobby, play some sport"" and I always wanted to. Actually I was very sporty kid in 1st and 2nd grade, regurairly running and having not only one of the best grades but also one of the best PE results among other kids. It was even my mom who first said that I could be a sporstman one day. But when she realised that I am also somehow intelectually gifted, she became too ambitious of me and REFUSED me to go and play some sport. (which was another seemingly weird thing about me as everyone in this country is pretty sports based)
When everything that holds me above a water at this points are video games and I feel less and less energised I wouldn't admit to myself that something was wrong
title: I just though to myself that I wad just probably getting hit by a puberty and that I was just sad. Because of being so addicted to games
body: I started missing school last year (1st grade in the middle school at this point). Of course my dad didn't like that at all and told me that he will either fully confiscate my PC or we will make a deal: I will go to school properly
label: I will be getting better grades and I can play all day I want. The choice was obvious.

id: Actually
title: that helped me unforeseemingly. Because there was something to push me forward like that
body: I didn't miss a single day in the school and my grades started to get really better for the upcoming month. I even started to play less as I was more interested in other things. But of course having that assurance that after a long day of school (I get off home by 5A.M. and come home by 5P.M.) I could get home and go relax with my online mates was very nice.

id: But because of what a fucking stoned jerk my dad was and still is, he FORGET (?!) about our deal and took my PC, fucking destroyed it to pieces while I was at school. I asked him what the fuck and that if we had a deal or not. He told me that he destroyed it because it was making a fan noises (which he should not care about since he shouldn't be in my room anyway) and that ""I shouldn't come on him with some deal bullcrap or anything like that"". Well I was shocked.
Since there was again nothing to keep going for
title: my mood started to get even progresivelly worse. I started to drop out of school even more and I started to notice that sometimes I would just lay in my bed
body: stare to the wall and think about all that shit and not have any energy to make anything other than that. That's when I started to be worried about myself.

id: Last week
title: we were with our mother for holiday and since I am a 15y.o. intelligent human being
body: I saw it as a good opportunity to talk to the only person I really trust and that was my mom.

id: So I started to tell her about the things that happened. (Including the moment, when I was preparing for the party we had in school in the 7th grade. I was really confident about myself as I had some really nice looking outfit in the long time and that I was suppossed to dance with my fiancГ©, which I was really hyped for. When I was dressing, I asked my mom if I should do my hair like this or that. She was like ""so that's how you're now"" and literally slapped me. So yeah that fucking ruined my confidence again and the whole evening for me. That probably again pissed her off and started to be rather annoyed, yet I just wanted to talk with clear heads). After that I told her that I don't feel very well in the past and I may be getting some mental health problems lately. She was like ""Depressions, depressions, come on"" and that was the end of the conversation.
That proved to me that my family isn't a good place to reach for things like that. But because of the situation
title: that was my last hope and I don't know what to do anymore and really I don't want to really. Whenever I though that I know what being hopeless is like
body: now I know it wasn't it. This is what it feels like. Even my chest feels hard when I try to think about what to do next.

id: Edit: also I stopped going to school entirely and I don't reach out to my friends about what happened since I feel really embarassed about the whole thing and even kinda guilty. I just don't know, what I should've tell them. ",1.0

id: 41,I'm done working for free for an asshole that has no respect for me.,"Five weeks ago, me and someone from my college (who'll I'll refer to as H) started at a work placement. It's a required part of my engineering course. It's a small business (3 guys, not including me and H) that manufactures signs. It's mostly unskilled manual labour.
Three weeks ago
title: H started being paid for his work placement. It's ВЈ5 an hour. He told me as soon as he started being paid. I found it unfair that he was being paid
body: and I wasn't. Bear in mind
label: both me and H work hard
column5: and are competent at our jobs. So I asked the boss if I could start earning a wage for working there. He said that because February isn't that busy for his business
column6: so he wouldn't start paying me (basically saying that he didn't need me).

id: This next bit may sound like a pathetic thing to complain about
title: but hear me out. Yesterday
body: one the guys who work there
label: whistled to me to get me to help him move a load of heavy stuff. I thought nothing of it
column5: until another one of the guys who work there told him to use my name. That was the point that I realised just how shitty it was. I'm working here (just want to emphasise
column6: for FREE) and they can't even be bothered to treat me with a bit of respect.

id: Now onto the bit that really pissed me off. A couple of hours ago
title: when I got into work
body: H told me that the boss had told him that I wasn't even supposed to know he was getting paid. That was supposed to remain a secret between H and the boss. That was the point that it went from being shitty
label: to having absolutely zero respect for me.

id: So that's it. I've got enough self respect to know that I can't keep coming into work for 8 hours a day
title: doing inpaid back breaking manual labour
body: whilst being treat like utter shit.  I'm making this guy money
label: by making signs that he then sells
column5: and yet I leave work in pain from working all day
column7: I know that people aren't automaticly  entitled to respect
column9: I won't work for someone who treats me like a mug.

id: Yesterday
title: my college tutor came to the work place to have a look around
body: and I told him that I wad done working there. That was before I found out that I wasn't even supposed to know about H getting paid.

id: However
title: the future is looking up. I've got an interview for a welding and fabrication course at a different college next month.

id: Moral of the story, have a bit of self respect, and don't work for people who don't respect you.",0.0

I dont really know how to change the way the see me since i've never even seen them in my entire life.",0.0
